API Reference¶

Application factory for the phone management backend.

app.__init__.create_app(config: AppConfig | None = None) → Flask

Creates and configures the Flask application.

Domain dataclasses and SQLAlchemy models.

class app.models.AuditLog(**kwargs)¶

Bases: Model

Audit trail tracking important business events.

created_at¶
event_type¶
id¶
message¶
query: t.ClassVar[Query]¶

A SQLAlchemy query for a model. Equivalent to db.session.query(Model). Can be customized per-model by overriding query_class.

Warning

The query interface is considered legacy in SQLAlchemy. Prefer using session.execute(select()) instead.

username¶
class app.models.PhoneStatus(value, names=<not given>, *values, module=None, qualname=None, type=None, start=1, boundary=None)¶

Bases: str, Enum

Enumerates the permitted phone lifecycle statuses.

DISPOSED = 'disposed'¶
ONLINE = 'online'¶
SOLD = 'sold'¶
STOLEN = 'stolen'¶
class app.models.PhoneStatusHistory(**kwargs)¶

Bases: Model

Historical phone status entries per user.

id¶
noted_at¶
query: t.ClassVar[Query]¶

A SQLAlchemy query for a model. Equivalent to db.session.query(Model). Can be customized per-model by overriding query_class.

Warning

The query interface is considered legacy in SQLAlchemy. Prefer using session.execute(select()) instead.

status¶
user_id¶
class app.models.PhoneStatusRecord(username: str, status: ~app.models.PhoneStatus, noted_at: ~datetime.datetime = <factory>)¶

Bases: object

Dataclass capturing a phone status entry for auditing.

noted_at: datetime¶
status: PhoneStatus¶
username: str¶
class app.models.Role(value, names=<not given>, *values, module=None, qualname=None, type=None, start=1, boundary=None)¶

Bases: str, Enum

Represents the access level of a system user.

ADMIN = 'admin'¶
MANAGER = 'manager'¶
USER = 'user'¶
class app.models.User(**kwargs)¶

Bases: UserMixin, Model

Persistent database representation of a user.

created_at¶
email¶
email_verification_token¶
id¶
imei¶
is_email_verified¶
password_hash¶
phone_number¶
query: t.ClassVar[Query]¶

A SQLAlchemy query for a model. Equivalent to db.session.query(Model). Can be customized per-model by overriding query_class.

Warning

The query interface is considered legacy in SQLAlchemy. Prefer using session.execute(select()) instead.

role¶
statuses¶
two_factor_secret¶
username¶
validate_username(_key: str, username: str) → str¶

Ensures usernames adhere to the defined constraints.

class app.models.UserProfile(username: str, email: str, phone_number: str, imei: str, role: Role = Role.USER)¶

Bases: object

Dataclass representing validated user metadata.

email: str¶
imei: str¶
phone_number: str¶
role: Role = 'user'¶
username: str¶

Security utilities including password hashing and 2FA.

exception app.services.security.HashingError¶

Bases: Exception

Raised when password hashing or verification fails.

exception app.services.security.PasswordComplexityError¶

Bases: Exception

Raised when a password does not meet complexity requirements.

class app.services.security.PasswordHasherProtocol(*args, **kwargs)¶

Bases: Protocol

Protocol describing the password hasher interface.

hash(password: str) → str¶
verify(hash: str, password: str) → bool¶
class app.services.security.SecurityService(hasher: PasswordHasherProtocol | None = None)¶

Bases: object

Provides password hashing, complexity validation, and 2FA utilities.

generate_two_factor_secret() → str¶

Generates a random secret for TOTP-based 2FA.

Returns:

Base32-encoded random secret for TOTP.

Return type:

str

hash_password(password: str) → str¶

Hashes the provided password using Argon2.

Parameters:

password (str) – Password to hash. Must meet complexity requirements.

Returns:

Argon2 password hash.

Return type:

str

Raises:

  • PasswordComplexityError – If password does not meet complexity requirements.

  • HashingError – If password hashing fails.

validate_password_complexity(password: str) → None¶

Validates that a password meets complexity requirements.

Password must contain: - At least 8 characters (max 128) - At least one lowercase letter - At least one uppercase letter - At least one digit - At least one special character (@$!%*?&)

Parameters:

password (str) – Password to validate.

Raises:

PasswordComplexityError – If password does not meet requirements.

verify_password(hash_value: str, password: str) → bool¶

Verifies the given password against the stored hash.

Parameters:

  • hash_value (str) – Stored password hash.

  • password (str) – Password to verify.

Returns:

True if password matches hash, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

Raises:

HashingError – If password verification fails.

Authentication and authorization service layer.

exception app.services.auth.AccountLockedError¶

Bases: AuthError

Raised when a user account is locked due to security policy.

exception app.services.auth.AuthError¶

Bases: Exception

Raised when authentication operations fail.

class app.services.auth.AuthService(security_service: SecurityService | None = None, audit_service: SecurityAuditService | None = None)¶

Bases: object

Handles registration, login, and authorization logic with security auditing.

This service enforces account lockout policies after repeated failed login attempts and logs all authentication-related security events.

authenticate(username: str, password: str) → User¶

Authenticates a user by verifying their credentials.

Enforces account lockout after repeated failed attempts and logs all authentication events for audit trail.

Parameters:

  • username (str) – Username to authenticate.

  • password (str) – Password to verify.

Returns:

Authenticated user object.

Return type:

User

Raises:

  • AccountLockedError – If account is locked.

  • AuthError – If authentication fails.

static load_user_by_id(user_id: str) → User | None¶

Loads a user for Flask-Login by identifier.

Parameters:

user_id (str) – User ID to load.

Returns:

User object if found, None otherwise.

Return type:

User | None

register_user(username: str, email: str, phone_number: str, imei: str, password: str, role: Role = Role.USER) → User¶

Registers a new user with validated profile data.

Parameters:

  • username (str) – Desired username.

  • email (str) – User email address.

  • phone_number (str) – User phone number.

  • imei (str) – Device IMEI.

  • password (str) – User password (must meet complexity requirements).

  • role (Role) – Initial user role. Defaults to Role.USER.

Returns:

Newly registered user object.

Return type:

User

Raises:

  • AuthError – If registration fails (duplicate username/email).

  • PasswordComplexityError – If password does not meet requirements.

require_roles(user: UserMixin, allowed_roles: Iterable[Role]) → None¶

Ensures the current user possesses one of the allowed roles.

Parameters:

  • user (UserMixin) – User to check.

  • allowed_roles (Iterable[Role]) – Roles that are permitted.

Raises:

AuthorizationError – If user does not have required role.

verify_totp(user: User, token: str) → bool¶

Verifies the provided TOTP token using the stored secret.

Parameters:

  • user (User) – User object with 2FA secret.

  • token (str) – TOTP token to verify.

Returns:

True if token is valid, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

exception app.services.auth.AuthorizationError¶

Bases: Exception

Raised when authorization checks fail.

class app.services.auth.PhoneStatusService¶

Bases: object

Manages phone status transitions and audit logging.

log_status(user: User, status: PhoneStatus) → None¶

Appends a phone status history entry for the given user.

Parameters:

  • user (User) – User for whom the status is being logged.

  • status (PhoneStatus) – Status value to persist.

Security audit logging service for tracking security events.

exception app.services.auditing.AuditingError¶

Bases: Exception

Raised when audit logging operations fail.

class app.services.auditing.SecurityAuditService¶

Bases: object

Logs security-related events for audit trails and monitoring.

This service provides centralized tracking of security events including failed login attempts, role changes, and administrative actions.

static clear_failed_login_attempts(username: str) → None¶

Clears failed login tracking for a user after successful login.

Parameters:

username (str) – Username whose failed login tracking should be cleared.

Raises:

AuditingError – If clearing fails.

static get_failed_login_count(username: str) → int¶

Retrieves the count of recent failed login attempts for a user.

Parameters:

username (str) – Username to check.

Returns:

Number of failed login attempts in the tracking window.

Return type:

int

static log_account_locked(username: str) → None¶

Logs when an account is locked due to failed login attempts.

Parameters:

username (str) – Username whose account was locked.

Raises:

AuditingError – If audit logging fails.

static log_failed_login(username: str, reason: str = 'Invalid credentials') → None¶

Logs a failed login attempt.

Parameters:

  • username (str) – Username that failed to authenticate.

  • reason (str) – Reason for the failure. Defaults to “Invalid credentials”.

Raises:

AuditingError – If audit logging fails.

static log_role_change(acting_user: str, target_user: str, new_role: str, old_role: str) → None¶

Logs when a user’s role is modified by an administrator.

Parameters:

  • acting_user (str) – Username performing the role change.

  • target_user (str) – Username whose role was changed.

  • new_role (str) – New role value.

  • old_role (str) – Previous role value.

Raises:

AuditingError – If audit logging fails.

static log_successful_login(username: str) → None¶

Logs a successful login event.

Parameters:

username (str) – Username that successfully authenticated.

Raises:

AuditingError – If audit logging fails.

static log_unauthorized_access_attempt(username: str | None, resource: str, reason: str) → None¶

Logs unauthorized access attempts.

Parameters:

  • username (Optional[str]) – Username attempting access (may be None).

  • resource (str) – Resource being accessed.

  • reason (str) – Reason access was denied.

Raises:

AuditingError – If audit logging fails.

Request payload validation utilities and data models.

class app.services.validation.LoginData(username: str, password: str, token: str | None)¶

Bases: object

Validated payload for login requests.

password: str¶
token: str | None¶
username: str¶
class app.services.validation.RegisterData(username: str, email: str, phone_number: str, imei: str, password: str, role: Role)¶

Bases: object

Validated payload for user registration requests.

email: str¶
imei: str¶
password: str¶
phone_number: str¶
role: Role¶
username: str¶
class app.services.validation.RoleUpdateData(role: Role)¶

Bases: object

Validated payload for role update requests.

role: Role¶
class app.services.validation.StatusUpdateData(status: PhoneStatus)¶

Bases: object

Validated payload for user status update requests.

status: PhoneStatus¶
class app.services.validation.UserUpdateData(email: str | None, phone_number: str | None, imei: str | None, role: Role | None)¶

Bases: object

Validated payload for administrative user updates.

email: str | None¶
imei: str | None¶
phone_number: str | None¶
role: Role | None¶
exception app.services.validation.ValidationError¶

Bases: Exception

Raised when incoming request payloads fail validation.

class app.services.validation.ValidationService¶

Bases: object

Provides helpers for validating and coercing incoming payloads.

static ensure_json_payload(payload: Mapping[str, Any] | None) → Mapping[str, Any]¶

Ensures a JSON payload is present for the request.

static parse_login_payload(payload: Mapping[str, Any] | None) → LoginData¶

Parses and validates login payload content.

static parse_register_payload(payload: Mapping[str, Any] | None) → RegisterData¶

Parses and validates registration payload content.

static parse_role_payload(payload: Mapping[str, Any] | None) → RoleUpdateData¶

Parses and validates administrative role update payloads.

static parse_status_payload(payload: Mapping[str, Any] | None) → StatusUpdateData¶

Parses and validates phone status update payloads.

static parse_user_update_payload(payload: Mapping[str, Any] | None) → UserUpdateData¶

Parses and validates user metadata update payloads.

Authentication-related endpoints.

app.routes.auth.login() → tuple[dict[str, object], int]¶

Authenticates the user and initiates a session.

app.routes.auth.logout() → tuple[dict[str, object], int]¶

Logs out the current session user.

app.routes.auth.register() → tuple[dict[str, object], int]¶

Registers a new application user.

User-facing endpoints for managing phone statuses.

app.routes.user.get_profile() → tuple[dict[str, object], int]¶

Returns the authenticated user’s profile.

app.routes.user.get_status_history() → tuple[dict[str, object], int]¶

Returns the authenticated user’s phone status history.

Returns:

Status history payload and

HTTP status code.

Return type:

tuple[dict[str, object], int]

app.routes.user.get_status_options() → tuple[dict[str, object], int]¶

Returns the allowable phone status values for selection.

Returns:

Allowed statuses and HTTP status code.

Return type:

tuple[dict[str, object], int]

app.routes.user.update_status() → tuple[dict[str, object], int]¶

Updates the user’s phone status.

Manager and admin-facing endpoints.

app.routes.manager.get_user(username: str) → tuple[dict[str, object], int]¶

Returns the profile and history for a specific user.

Parameters:

username (str) – Username to retrieve.

Returns:

User detail payload and status code.

Return type:

tuple[dict[str, object], int]

app.routes.manager.list_user_statuses(username: str) → tuple[dict[str, object], int]¶

Provides status history for a user.

Parameters:

username (str) – Username whose statuses are requested.

Returns:

Status history payload and status code.

Return type:

tuple[dict[str, object], int]

app.routes.manager.list_users() → tuple[dict[str, object], int]¶

Lists user profiles for manager viewing.

Returns:

Serialized users payload and status code.

Return type:

tuple[dict[str, object], int]

app.routes.manager.update_user(username: str) → tuple[dict[str, object], int]¶

Updates user details as an admin.

Parameters:

username (str) – Username to update.

Returns:

Updated user payload and status code.

Return type:

tuple[dict[str, object], int]

app.routes.manager.update_user_role(username: str) → tuple[dict[str, object], int]¶

Allows admins to promote or demote user roles.

Parameters:

username (str) – Username whose role is being modified.

Returns:

Response message and status code.

Return type:

tuple[dict[str, object], int]

Helper utilities for consistent API responses.

app.routes.responses.error_response(message: str, status_code: int) → tuple[dict[str, object], int]¶

Constructs an error response payload.

Parameters:

  • message (str) – Error message to include in the response.

  • status_code (int) – HTTP status code representing the error.

Returns:

Error payload and HTTP status code.

Return type:

tuple[dict[str, object], int]

app.routes.responses.validation_error_response(message: str) → tuple[dict[str, object], int]¶

Constructs a validation error response payload.

Parameters:

message (str) – Validation error message to include in the response.

Returns:

Error payload and HTTP 400 status code.

Return type:

tuple[dict[str, object], int]
